# suggest: KG dangling-WARN flags relation-only classes (TC/CR/SR); #252 item(3) persists on 0.11.2

## Summary

KG dangling-WARN structurally over-reports relation-only entity classes and persists on 0.11.2 after CataForge#252 (which raised it as item 3) was closed COMPLETED on 2026-06-12. The dangling scan _scan_fs_entity_ids builds its "referenced" set with the full entity-prefix regex, including TC (TestCase) / CR (CoverageRule) / SR (SprintReviewIssue) prefixes whose classes are relation-only — the importer never materialises them as entity nodes. So any active doc that merely lists those ids is guaranteed to be flagged dangling regardless of how it is written; a test-report coverage matrix alone carries 71 TC ids, and on 0.11.2 cataforge doctor still reports 76 (71 TC + 4 CR + 1 SR). The only downstream workaround — wrapping all 71 TC ids in inline code — mangles the coverage matrix and is a hack, not a real fix. Proposed fix: restrict the dangling check's "referenced" candidate set to prefixes whose class is actually entity-ised, removing the candidate-set vs node-set asymmetry. This was already characterised as item (3) of CataForge#252 but remained unimplemented when that issue was closed COMPLETED.

## Environment

- **CataForge package**: `0.11.2`
- **Scaffold version**: `0.11.2`
- **Python**: `3.14.4`
- **Platform**: `Windows-11-10.0.26200-SP0`
- **Runtime platform**: `claude-code`

## Proposal

Prior filing: docs/feedback/feedback-suggest-kg-ingest-gate-legacy-docs-20260612.md -> CataForge#252 (CLOSED/COMPLETED 2026-06-12). Items (1) migration ergonomics and (2) importer define-once heuristic appear addressed; item (3) dangling-scan relation-only asymmetry is the residual, verified persisting on package 0.11.2 / scaffold 0.11.2 this session.
